g.text: 0x60 | uint64_t | total_bits // Total input bits seen (all update() calls)
0x68 | size_t | buf_len // Valid bytes in buf: range [0, 63]
Total: 108 bytes (+ padding to align size_t on target ABI)
Step 1 | buf[buf_len] = 0x80 | append marker immediately after last message byte
Step 2 | used = buf_len + 1 | bytes occupied including marker
Step 3 | if used > 56: compress buf | not enough room for 8-byte length field
Step 3b| memset(buf, 0, 64) | fresh block for length-only second block
Step 4 | else: memset(buf+used, 0, 56-used) | zero fill within same block
Step 5 | buf[56..63] = total_bits BE | 8-byte big-endian original bit count
Step 6 | sha256_compress(H, buf) | final compression of padded block
Step 7 | digest[i*4..i*4+3] = H[i] BE | 8 x uint32_t -> 32 bytes big-endian
Output | 64-char hex via nibble table | HEX[(byte>>4)&0xF], HEX[byte&0xF]
Phase 1 | buf_len > 0 | fill partial buf; compress if buf_len reaches 64
Phase 2 | i+64 <= len | compress complete blocks DIRECTLY from data (no copy)
Phase 3 | remaining > 0 | memcpy tail bytes into buf; set buf_len = remaining
total_bits updated FIRST | += len * 8 at top of function before any phases
Thread safety | NOT thread-safe; one ctx per thread or external lock
After block N | = sha256_compress output | += a..h from 64 rounds
After finalize() | = final digest words | written big-endian to digest[]
Size | 8 × 4 = 32 bytes | fits in 4 XMM registers (SHA-NI)
buf[0..buf_len-1] | message data | copied in by update()
buf[buf_len] | 0x80 marker byte | written by finalize()
buf[buf_len+1..55] | 0x00 zero fill | memset by finalize()
buf[56..63] | 64-bit BE length | total_bits, written by finalize()
Extra block case | buf_len >= 56 | full buf compressed, fresh buf for length
sha256_update(N) | total_bits += N * 8 | accumulate across all calls
sha256_finalize() | written to buf[56..63] BE | FIPS 180-4 length field
Max value | 2^64 - 1 bits = ~2 EB | sufficient for any real input
Encoding | buf[56] = bits >> 56 ... | explicit byte-by-byte, big-endian
update() phase 1 | buf_len += copied | fill from previous partial
update() flush | buf_len = 0 | reset after full-block compress
update() phase 3 | buf_len = remain | store tail bytes
Invariant: end of update()| 0 <= buf_len <= 63| never == 64 (flushed eagerly)
finalize(): buf_len < 56 | one final block | marker + zeros + length
finalize(): buf_len >= 56 | TWO final blocks | boundary case: 56-byte input
context lifecycle: init → update* → finalize → init
